panels.title: Recent failures
panels.description: Most recent 100 error events. The user, tool, error_code, and message are everything you need to chase a failure.

targets.rawSql: SELECT occurred_at AS "time", host_user AS "user", event_type AS "tool", error_code, error_message, scope_shot, scope_asset FROM events WHERE status = 'error' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) ORDER BY occurred_at DESC LIMIT 100

panels.title: Errors by tool
panels.description: Per-tool reliability. Shows raw errors and the error rate against total runs so a low-volume flaky tool doesn't get drowned out by a high-volume one with rare failures. The trend column is errors per hour over the dashboard time range.

targets.rawSql: SELECT event_type AS "tool", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "errors", COUNT(*) AS "runs", CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') / COUNT(*), 1) ELSE 0 END AS "err rate", COUNT(DISTINCT host_user) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "users", COUNT(DISTINCT error_code) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "codes", MAX(occurred_at) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "last failure" FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY event_type HAVING COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') > 0 ORDER BY "errors" DESC
SELECT date_trunc('hour', occurred_at) AS "time", event_type AS "tool", COUNT(*) AS value FROM events WHERE status = 'error' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Errors by user
panels.description: Per-user reliability. The 'top error code' column counts the most-frequent error_code per user — spots someone hitting the same bug repeatedly, vs an even spread across many issues.

targets.rawSql: WITH stats AS (SELECT COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>') AS "user", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "errors", COUNT(*) AS "runs", COUNT(DISTINCT event_type) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "tools w/ errors", MAX(occurred_at) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "last failure" FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>')), top_codes AS (SELECT COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>') AS "user", COALESCE(error_code, 'UNKNOWN') || ' (' || COUNT(*) || ')' AS "top error code", ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>') ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rn FROM events WHERE status = 'error' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>'), error_code) SELECT s."user", s."errors", s."runs", CASE WHEN s."runs" > 0 THEN ROUND(100.0 * s."errors" / s."runs", 1) ELSE 0 END AS "err rate", s."tools w/ errors", COALESCE(tc."top error code", '-') AS "top error code", s."last failure" FROM stats s LEFT JOIN top_codes tc ON tc."user" = s."user" AND tc.rn = 1 WHERE s."errors" > 0 ORDER BY s."errors" DESC

panels.title: Errors by error_code
panels.description: Failure modes ranked by frequency. The sample-message column is what makes UNKNOWN rows useful — without it you'd have to bounce to Recent failures to read the actual exception. The trend column is occurrences per hour.

targets.rawSql: WITH stats AS (SELECT COALESCE(error_code, 'UNKNOWN') AS "error_code", COUNT(*) AS "count", COUNT(DISTINCT event_type) AS "tools", COUNT(DISTINCT host_user) AS "users", MAX(occurred_at) AS "last seen" FROM events WHERE status = 'error' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(error_code, 'UNKNOWN')), recent_msg AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (COALESCE(error_code, 'UNKNOWN')) COALESCE(error_code, 'UNKNOWN') AS "error_code", error_message AS "sample error_message" FROM events WHERE status = 'error' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) ORDER BY COALESCE(error_code, 'UNKNOWN'), occurred_at DESC) SELECT s."error_code", s."count", s."tools", s."users", s."last seen", COALESCE(rm."sample error_message", '') AS "sample error_message" FROM stats s LEFT JOIN recent_msg rm USING ("error_code") ORDER BY s."count" DESC

SELECT date_trunc('hour', occurred_at) AS "time", COALESCE(error_code, 'UNKNOWN') AS "error_code", COUNT(*) AS value FROM events WHERE status = 'error' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Events per user
panels.description: Per-user usage profile (all statuses). The 'favorite tool' column shows each user's most-used event_type with its run count — the fastest answer to 'who do I ask about feature X?'

targets.rawSql: WITH usage AS (SELECT COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>') AS "user", COUNT(*) AS "runs", COUNT(DISTINCT event_type) AS "tools used", MAX(occurred_at) AS "last activity" FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>')), favorite AS (SELECT COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>') AS "user", event_type || ' (' || COUNT(*) || ')' AS "favorite tool", ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>') ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rn FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>'), event_type) SELECT u."user", u."runs", u."tools used", COALESCE(f."favorite tool", '-') AS "favorite tool", u."last activity" FROM usage u LEFT JOIN favorite f ON f."user" = u."user" AND f.rn = 1 ORDER BY u."runs" DESC

panels.title: Tools used per user
panels.description: For each (user, tool) pair: how many runs and how many errors. Spot tools that one user hits but nobody else exercises.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(host_user, '<unknown>') AS "user", event_type AS "tool", count(*) AS "runs", count(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'error') AS "errors" FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY host_user, event_type ORDER BY "runs" DESC
